entry.title: C_IEE2E Practical Exam Under Review → Attempt Closed & Recredited – Has Anyone Experienced This?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to check if anyone else has experienced a similar situation with the new system-based practical certification exams, particularly C_IEE2E – SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – End-to-End Business Processes for SAP Business Suite.

My situation:

  • I completed the C_IEE2E practical exam on 25 January 2026.

  • The exam status remained “Under Review” for several days (despite results usually being available shortly after completion).

  • I contacted SAP Certification Support.

  • I later received a response stating that:

    • They cannot provide detailed information on why the attempt was unsuccessful.

    • The certification is currently in a pilot phase, and detailed score reports are not yet available.

    • My attempt has been closed and recredited.

While I appreciate the recredit, I am now a bit unsure about the next best step.

My questions to the community and SAP team:

  1. Has anyone else faced a similar situation with C_IEE2E or other system-based practical exams?

  2. If your attempt was recredited, did you:

    • Retake the same exam immediately?

    • Wait for process improvements or clearer guidance?

  3. Are there known stability or evaluation issues with the pilot practical exams that candidates should be aware of?

  4. Would you recommend proceeding with a retake now, or waiting until the enhanced score reporting process is fully available?

Additionally, I am planning to take the following certifications soon:

  • SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting

  • SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Management Accounting

Given this experience, I would really value advice on whether it’s better to:

  • Continue with these certifications now, or

  • Pause until the practical exam processes are more mature.

I’m sharing this to learn from others’ experiences and to get guidance from SAP experts or community members who may have gone through something similar.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: KW407: No prices could be determined for Activity Typ...
entry.content:
Good day @HuntonDavi11094 

Thank you for your question.

That error highlights that costing run did not find any valid activity price or cost rate for the specific combination of controlling area, your cost area, activity type and the planning context your valuation variant strategy is trying to use.

Note that uploading planned costs through import Financial Plan Data and planned quantities through Service Planning does not automatically guarantee that an activity price exists for costing. So, depending on your valuation strategy, the system might require:

  • Plan cost rate you manually maintained or
  • A cost rate that is calculated by the system. See Cost Center Activity Cost Rate Calculation for more insights.

If neither of the two is available in the ways the sequence expects it, the error you got reflects.

If neither is available in the way the strategy sequence expects, you get KW407.

Additionally, I suggest you:

  1. Validate your valuation variant strategy sequence used for activity prices. Make sure you are not using a plant specific variant that has higher priority than the global one. Your plan category must be PLN for the valuation variant. You mentioned PLN, still confirm it is set in the valuation variant used by your plant. See KBA 3318691 for more details.
  2. Your strategy might try to average the whole fiscal year, which requires the cost center to be valid on the fiscal year start date. If the cost center validity starts later in the year, the system can fail to fetch the rate and still display KW407. That is a known restriction in public cloud. In that case, i suggest you:
  • Change the strategy to plan price for the period, or
  • Extend the cost center validity so it is valid from the fiscal start year date. KBA 3589260 provides some insights.
  1. Ensure you have a cost rate in the correct place for your scenario. Because of your cost center category is C, also check whether you should maintain cost rates with the cost rates framework apps. See See Manage Cost Rates - Services and Manage Cost Rates - Plan for more details

To answer your questions:

Are there any additional prerequisites for activity price determination in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Yes, the key ones are:

  • The costing variant must point to a valuation variant whose activity strategy sequence can actually find a rate. This was elaborated above.
  • If the strategy averages the whole year, the cost center validity must cover the fiscal year start date. Also elaborated above.
  • A plan cost rate must exist either through manual maintenance or through system price calculation approach but depends on your strategy.

Are there known restrictions where planned activity prices are not read during costing?

Yes, the most common one in public cloud is the Average of all fiscal year periods strategy combined with cost center validity not starting at fiscal year start, which can then cause the error KW407 you experienced even when you think rates exist. Also, if you have maintained a plant specific valuation variant, it overrides the global valuation variant and can point to a different strategy than you expect.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Planned Independent Requirements for determining targ...
entry.content:
Hello @AngelT

Based on your screenshot, your PIR exist. So, the error you are seeing is not about the quantities you maintained. You seem to have a key mismatch. In your case, the most likely mismatch is MRP Area. PIRs are on MRP  Area 5540, while Rapid Replenishment is reading plant level. Add 5540 to MRP Area run and re test, then confirm it through message details.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Student Competition 2026
entry.content:
We are excited to announce the SAP Analytics Cloud Student Competition 2026, an analytics challenge open to universities across Latin America, Spain, and Portugal. Taking place in March 2026, this competition is designed to foster creativity, analytical thinking, and hands-on experience through the development of business dashboards using SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC).

This initiative brings together students and professors to apply real-world analytics concepts, transform data into insights, and showcase innovative data storytelling skills.

 

Who Can Participate?

  • Teams of 4 to 5 undergraduate or graduate students

  • Each team must be led by one or two professors

  • Participation is open to universities from Latin America, Spain, and Portugal

  • Registrations must be completed individually

 

Challenge Focus

Participants will work with the Global Bike 4.3 dataset, analyzing key business areas such as sales, costs, and product performance.
The main objective is to design innovative and insightful business dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud, using comparative and trend-based visualizations and key metrics such as revenue, costs, and margins.

 

Registration Information

To register, teams must provide:

  • Team Name

  • University

  • Lead Professor (full name)

  • Lead Professor’s Email Address

  • Assistant/Co-Professor (optional)

Registration deadline:
Sunday, March 1st, 2026
(Registrations will not be accepted after this date.)

 

Event Structure & Key Dates

SAP Analytics Cloud Workshop

March 9, 10, and 11

Hands-on training sessions focused on:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud fundamentals

  • Data modeling

  • Dashboard design best practices

These sessions provide a solid technical foundation to prepare participants for the challenge.
Recommended level: intermediate knowledge of SAC.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Challenge – Opening Session

March 13

A session exclusively for professors, aimed at:

  • Presenting the competition objectives and rules

  • Explaining evaluation criteria

  • Introducing the Global Bike 4.3 dataset

  • Clarifying expectations and the professor’s role as facilitator

This session ensures professors are fully aligned and prepared to guide and support their student teams throughout the competition.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud Competition Day

March 18

Official start of the competition.
Teams will:

  • Build and submit their dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud

  • Apply analytics, creativity, and storytelling to solve the challenge

Awards ceremony will take place on the same day.

 

Evaluation Criteria

Dashboards will be evaluated based on:

  • Analytical quality

  • Clarity of insights and communication

  • Creative and effective use of SAP Analytics Cloud tools

In addition, the SAP University Alliances community will participate by voting on dashboards, focusing on visual design and presentation.

 

Certificates & Recognition

  • All participants will receive a Certificate of Participation

  • Members of the top three winning teams will receive an official Digital Badge

 

Join Us!

This competition is a unique opportunity for students and professors to demonstrate analytical expertise, creativity, and innovation using SAP Analytics Cloud in an academic and collaborative environment.

We look forward to seeing your ideas come to life and wish all participants the best of luck!

Register here

Let’s shape the future of analytics together.

entry.title: Re: Where-used list of method with code line
entry.content: You may use the class CL_ABAP_COMPILER to know the exact line (and other CL_ABAP_COMP_* classes). It's parsing the source code. You may use for instance the method GET_SINGLE_REF to find out at which line(s) a desired method YOURCLASS=>YOURMETHOD is invoked, and the parameter P_FULL_NAME will be \TY:YOURCLASS\ME:YOURMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: Need to know how can Return Revenue GL can be assigne...
entry.content:
Hello @abulhasnat

Regarding what “rule” you should use?

It is important to note that condition exclusion rules do not decide based on OR vs CBAR. They only decided after both conditions are found. So, you cannot solve “OR uses PPR0, CBAR uses ZINV” by an exclusion rule alone.

What you must do in standard pricing is to make sure only one of the two conditions is determined per scenario. Then the exclusion group becomes a safety net. The standard way is to differentiate by key fields in the condition record not BAdI.

I have some questions:

Are both of your condition types in the same pricing procedure?

Did you maintain condition records for PPR0 for standard categories?

Did you maintain condition records for ZINV only for the return item category that is used by CBAR?

 

Regarding LR2 --> G2, i suggest:

  • You stay with pricing based account key separation or you populate the custom field in billing by using Custom Logic.

Best regards

Chris

Re: Role assignment of Admin not automated as per Locatio...
entry.content: @DINTPC, I may not have fully understood the situation, but here’s my comment. When a Learning User who has a related Learning Administrator account is inactivated, their admin account is automatically locked (KBA 2430232). In general, IDs cannot be changed, and entities cannot be deleted due to system integrity and restrictions (see KBA 2253851).

entry.title: Multiple Openings on Requisition and separate positions: How are others handling this?
entry.content:
We currently use position management, as well as the recruiting module.  We  have a 1:1 relationship between position and employee (there are no multi-incumbent positions).

We do however post requisitions with multiple openings. We then create additional position numbers to account for the additional openings.  Example: Position 1200 could have 3 openings, so we would create position 1201,  and 1203.

When someone has been hired into the requisition, they would automatically be placed into position 1201, and then additional hires would need the position to be manually overridden during the onboarding/new hire data entry step.

Our Recruiting team is currently putting notes in the requisition comments section, to notify our HR Operations team on which position to use for a new hire, but the step is frequently getting missed, and we our HR Ops team doesn't always know which position to use. 

Does anyone else use this same practice, or have advice on how best handle this type of process?

entry.title: Re: The consequences of implementing strictly Shift-left architectures
entry.content:
@appel_solar_dk  Thanks again for your comments. Maybe to add that the article/thought experiment is not only about SAP products, but my intention was to be more general, as I work not only with SAP anymore and I observe quite some things that are not there but which I took for granted. I wrote an article about this some time ago, I can send it to you, it was about that BW is the gray panther of DWing. Now to your comments: CDS views could carry maybe more semantic, but just the fact that the names of both views and fields are so long and in many cases almost self-explaining is a lot of semantics (for an e.g. Agent, I tested this). Just this is much better than previous MATNR or BUKRS which for German speaker migt still give a chance but for many other much harder to understand than material or company code. it's true that associations are not passed through, and that would be really cool, if the VDM of S4 could be selectively transfered. 

About "Only data physically stored in the object store can be shared with databricks using data products", fully agree, that is a little bit thin and DSP-based "data products" would need to be stored again in the object store, which is not really zero-copy. 
About "The idea of governed and semantically rich data is fine, but there is quite a long way before we are there." I agree, especially if we look outside. The BW extractors were by then the most important reason for making BW successful in its early years, apart of being part of NW. Many DW failed by then if running against SAP R/3. Now we have a much better understandable extraction logic with built in delta mechanism (_DEX CDS) and that's beautiful, for me at least. Having to create by yourself multi-table CDC on business entity level can be quite cumbersome without such infrastructure, and only a few tools, DSP included offer such a mechanism.

The main intention was to sum up pro's and con's or possible inhibitors of considering shift left approach, not only for SAP scenarios. I hope I have achieved this. Kind regards

entry.title: Sample value drivers for effective Business Value Realization of your Public Cloud Solution
entry.content:
In this blog, I continue with the topic of Business Value Realization. If you are reading this blog for the first time and you have not read my value series, then I suggest you look at these two blogs first to understand the full concept of value realization using the help of SAP for Me for SAP Public Cloud Solutions: Drive more Business Value out of your SAP Public Cloud Solution Subscriptions and SAP for Me – Adoption Report – Drive More Business Value Realization.

Here, I would like to focus on a simple example of how an SAP Cloud ERP (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition) live customer can start with a quick, comprehensive analysis to add more business outcomes using some of the value drivers SAP provides in SAP for Me, with a high number of solution capabilities for SAP Cloud ERP.

Now, keep in mind these value drivers are not the only ones you can use, but I want to recommend them to you based on my experience and the fact they will help you improve multiple areas.

So for my example, I will use an SAP Cloud ERP customer utilizing finance, sales, procurement, logistics, and manufacturing.

Now, to simplify the analysis and example, I will only focus on two value drivers for the following improvement areas to make a comprehensive analysis:

  • Reduce selling, general, and administrative expenses
  • Increase revenue
  • Decrease cost of goods sold (COGS)
  • Improve working capital
  • Improve pre-tax margin

Sample value drivers by improvement area

Now, with these value drivers, I can go back to SAP for Me and use the Customer Insight Dashboard Cloud Functional Usage under the Business Value section to look at the solution areas my business can adapt to impact these value drivers more and bring greater business outcomes to your organization.

Let’s start looking at lowering our selling, general, and administrative expenses. Notice that after adding the value drivers “Reduce account receivable management costs” and “Reduce invoice error rate,” SAP for Me displays these results.

SAP for Me – Customer Insights Dashboard – Cloud Functional Usage – Business Value

Value Driver Selection - SAP for Me – Customer Insights Dashboard – Cloud Functional Usage – Business Value

Selling, general, and administrative expenses - “Reduce account receivable management costs” and “Reduce invoice error rate” value drivers

The customer is using 4 solution capabilities out of 10.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in selling, general and administrative expenses.

Our customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 6 additional solution capabilities. Of course, as previously discussed in my blogs, customers can compare the unused solution capabilities with the industry peer group to be more focused on the activation. All this information can be reached via SAP for Me.

Moving now to the Increase Revenue area and the value drivers “Reduce manufacturing cycle time” and “Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment Issues,” SAP for Me displays the results.

Revenue - “Reduce manufacturing cycle time” and “Reduce revenue loss due to fulfillment Issues” value drivers

The customer is using 12 solution capabilities out of 15.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in revenue

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 3 additional solution capabilities.  

Within the Decreased Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) section, you’ll notice that when value drivers like “Reduce total manufacturing costs” and “Reduce asset maintenance cost” are added, SAP for Me displays the corresponding results.

Cost of goods sold - “Reduce total manufacturing costs” and “Reduce asset maintenance cost” value drivers

The customer is using 10 solution capabilities out of 15.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in cost of goods sold

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 5 additional solution capabilities.

When you add the value drivers "Reduce sales days outstanding" and "Reduce days in inventory" to improve the Working Capital area, SAP for Me displays the resulting outcomes.

Working capital – “Reduce sales days outstanding" and "Reduce days in inventory" value drivers

The customer is using 13 solution capabilities out of 30.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in working capital

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 17 additional solution capabilities.

If you add the value drivers of “Improved on-time delivery” and “Reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs”, SAP for Me shows the following results for improving the Pre-Tax Margin area.

Pre-Tax Margin - “Improved on-time delivery” and “Reduce uncollectible accounts receivable write-offs” value drivers

The customer is using 20 solution capabilities out of 35.

Solutions capabilities for selected value drivers in pre-tax margin

The customer has an opportunity to activate and use a total of 15 additional solution capabilities.

Now, summing it all up, to create a comprehensive analysis on additional value realization, here is the final view using the recommended value driver by improvement area. As you can see from the graphic, the yellow area provides us with an additional opportunity for value creation using solution capabilities currently not used, which can bring new improvements and impact the value drivers.

Additional opportunity for value creation in selected improvement areas

Now, realistically speaking, implementing all these solution capabilities can be arduous, so here I recommend thinking about adding another strategy, such as filtering by benchmarking with your industry peers, to truly reduce the scope and only select the ones that are proven to be heavily used by your industry peers. If you would like to see how to do this, refer to my previous blog "Drive more Business Value out of your SAP Public Cloud Solution Subscriptions."

Now, this example can also apply to other public cloud subscriptions like SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Integrated Business Planning, etc., so feel free to play with the value drivers included in SAP for Me to find the areas of improvement you want to drive for your business.

Keep in mind that solution capabilities are expanding from traditional functional scope to also include SAP Business AI, so SAP will continue to add more in this area. As in my previous blogs, remember, this is not the only tool that can be used; SAP has other tools such as SAP Signavio, SAP WalkMe, SAP LeanIX, SAP Value Lifecycle Manager, and/or SAP Cloud ALM that can be used in tandem to help drive more business value from your SAP subscription.

I encourage you to discuss these points with your SAP Customer Success Manager, SAP Account Executive, or SAP Partner. They may be able to provide you with additional recommendations and guidance.

Your insights and opinions are valuable, so please don't hesitate to contribute by commenting on this blog.

Follow us via @SAP and #S4HANA or follow @Marco_Valencia (SAP Business Suite Customer Success Management).

entry.title: HU Management for batch-managed raw materials: tracking partial quantities per container
entry.content:
We are working with a batch-managed raw material that is purchased and stored in physical containers (12 kg buckets).

At goods receipt, the material is posted in kilograms, so SAP correctly shows total stock and available batches. However, we currently have no visibility of:

  • how many physical buckets exist,

  • how many kg remain in each bucket after partial consumption,

  • and where each individual bucket is located.

Production often consumes only a partial quantity from a bucket, leaving open containers in the warehouse with different remaining quantities.

The business requirement is to track each physical bucket individually (container-level visibility), while keeping batch management for traceability and expiry purposes.

Questions:

  1. Is HU (Handling Unit) Management the recommended approach for this scenario?

  2. Can HU management be activated only for specific storage locations while the material remains batch-managed?

  3. How does SAP handle partial consumption of an HU and what happens when the HU quantity reaches zero?

  4. Which configuration or design considerations should be taken into account (IM - EWM)?

Any best practices or real-life implementation guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Enabling WebSocket for Fiori Elements app
entry.content:
Hi,

I managed to resolve this. It was two different issues and none of them was related to network.

Issue #1: when ws connection is accepted, there is a check for origin. Hence when doing local development, host localhost:8080 does not get accepted. The exceptions can be maintained in transaction SAPC_CROSS_ORIGIN. This is documented in OSS note and mentioned in documentation as well. This resolved the problem with local development. 

Issue #2: I looked at the requests in developer tools and I noticed the familiar pattern. The request to oData service would look like hostname/<site ID>.<app name>/<cache ID>/sap/opu... while request for websocket looked like hostname/sap/bc/apc/... I remembered this issue that for Workzone site, the URL path for service must be defined as a relative path instead of absolute path. The same problem was here. I debugged the creation of WS connection in Fiori Elements and it determined service path in absolute format /sap/bc/apc/... I found in UI5 documentation that it's possbile to adjust the websocket path via annotation. After adding the following annotation via local file it started working. Please note that the path is defined as relative, it does not start with /.

<Annotations Target="SAP__self.Container">
  <Annotation Term="Common.WebSocketBaseURL" String="sap/bc/apc/sap/odata" />
</Annotations>

It works all now. But my question would be, is this again just a limitation of Workzone or is there a better way how to make websocket works in Fiori Elements.

entry.title: ECC to S/4HANA Migration(SD)
entry.content:
SD POV - Migration(Brown Field Implementation) : ECC to S/4HANA
Author:

Hari Boda is working as a Senior BW-HANA consultant at  HGS Pvt  Ltd  and has worked on multiple assignments in BW-HANA analytics  domain.

Brief:

With S/4 HANA is growing in SAP world there are many questions looming regarding the impact/changes and disruptions. This post mainly outlines the impact on traditional SAP ECC migration to ‘S/4 HANA ’  from SD point view.

 

ECC to S/4HANA Migration Overview (Brownfield Implementation) - SD POV
The ECC to S/4HANA migration project follows a Brownfield implementation approach and is executed in three distinct phases, each with clearly defined roles and responsibilities:

Pre-Migration Server

Technical Migration

S/4HANA Server (Post-Migration)

1. Pre-Migration Phase
The Pre-Migration phase focuses on system readiness and mandatory preparatory activities prior to the technical conversion. A major objective of this phase is the migration of Customers and Vendors to Business Partner (BP).

Key Activities Include:

Business Partner (BP) activation

Definition of BP roles

Definition of BP number ranges

Definition of BP groupings

Definition of field groupings

Assignment of customer account groups to BP roles

Assignment of customer account groups to BP groupings

Configuration of field mappings

Execution of the Upgrade Check Report

Execution of the Simplification Item Check Report

Migration of all Customers and Vendors to Business Partners (BP)

2. Technical Migration Phase
The Technical Migration phase is primarily handled by Basis and ABAP consultants. This phase involves system conversion, technical adjustments, and remediation of custom developments to ensure compatibility with S/4HANA.

3. Post-Migration (S/4HANA Server) Phase
The Post-Migration phase includes configuration and validation activities performed on the S/4HANA system after technical conversion.

Key Activities Include:

BP activation for BP-to-Customer direction

BP configuration for BP-to-Customer integration

Credit Management configuration

Migration of Credit Master data

Migration of Condition Master data

Configuration of Settlement Management

Configuration of BRF+ Output Management

Configuration of Advanced ATP (aATP)

Upgrade Check Program
Program Name: PRECHECK_UPGRADATION_REPORT

SD Delta Changes in S/4HANA
The following key changes in Sales and Distribution (SD) must be addressed during or after migration from ECC to S/4HANA:

VBUK/VBUP (Document and Item Status tables) are eliminated.
Status data is now stored in:

VBAK / VBAP (Sales Documents)

LIKP / LIPS (Deliveries)

VBRK (Billing Documents)

The number of user-defined condition tables has increased significantly (almost doubled).

Pricing table KONV has been replaced by PRCD_ELEMENTS.

Finance and Logistics Data Model Changes
MKPF/MSEG data is now stored in the MATDOC table.

BKPF/BSEG data is now stored in the ACDOCA table.

Master Data Note
There are no structural changes to Customer and Vendor master tables; however, they are now managed via the Business Partner framework.

Additional Notes:
T-code FD32 has been eliminated - T-code FD33 credit master is available.
T-codes XD01/XD02/XD03/XK01/XK02/XK03 has been eliminated instead we are going to use BP - T-code.

Credit management has been shifted to finance supply chain management with additional        functionalities.
Rebate management concept has been converted to settlement management.
Output determination has been converted to Business rule framework.
Many additional S/4HANA delta changes are available and will be addressed in detail in the next blog

entry.title: HU Management for batch-managed raw materials: tracking partial quantities per container
entry.content:
We are working with a batch-managed raw material that is purchased and stored in physical containers (12 kg buckets).

At goods receipt, the material is posted in kilograms, so SAP correctly shows total stock and available batches. However, we currently have no visibility of:
- How many physical buckets exist.
- How many kg remain in each bucket after partial consumption.
- Where each individual bucket is located.

Production often consumes only a partial quantity from a bucket, leaving open containers in the warehouse with different remaining quantities.
The business requirement is to track each physical bucket individually (container-level visibility), while keeping batch management for traceability and expiry purposes.

Questions:
- Is HU (Handling Unit) Management the recommended approach for this scenario?
- Can HU management be activated only for specific storage locations while the material remains batch-managed?
- How does SAP handle partial consumption of an HU and what happens when the HU quantity reaches zero?

Which configuration or design considerations should be taken into account (IM - WM)?

Any best practices or real-life implementation guidance would be greatly appreciated.

I still don't have authorizations on SAP Store, but we were able to access new applications on BTP, the issue was elsewhere. All solutions are available on SAP Marketplace, but the permission for entitlements on GlobalAccount must be given not only to the designated subaccount, but also the folder the subaccount is in. 

For instance, to provide access to 'Development CAP' inside 'CAP':

I have had to add the assignment in 'Entity Assignments' to both the subaccount and directory.

It has solved our issue and there was no need to access SAP Discovery Center.

entry.title: Quote 2.0 scripting: apply changes only to filtered rows/items in quote item grid?
entry.content:
Hi SAP CPQ community,

We are using SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 and want to apply a scripted update only to the items currently returned by the Quote UI “Filter Items” panel (Part Number / Product Type / Product Name filter)

Question:
Is there a supported way in Quote 2.0 to access the filtered result set (the rows/items currently shown after applying filters) from server-side scripting, so we can apply changes only to those items?

Context / what we tried:

  • In most cases our current logic applies changes across the quote using context.Quote.GetAllItems()
  • We tested whether the scripting context provides any “subset” collections (e.g., context.AffectedItems) but in our event context AffectedItems is not available (hasattr(context,"AffectedItems") == False)
  • We understand Quote 2.0 scripting is event-based and context objects vary by event.

If this isn’t possible directly from scripting, what’s the recommended pattern to “apply changes only to filtered lines” in Quote 2.0?

If there are only two values  in the dimension to a combined bar and line chart, you could use %GrandTotal to show both the actual number in each gender in the bars, plus the percentage as a line. However, if there are more than two values it doesn't work because it calculates each one based on the grand total. 

On the Formulas and Calculations page (link below), there's also a reference to a similar Subtotal option that theoretically should work, but I haven't been able to create a calculation using it. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/story-reports/all-formulas-and-calculations

entry.title: Variable Pay Basis Calculation does not populate following a change to Job Information in EC
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec

For our Variable Pay template, we run the following daily jobs to keep the employee data in variable pay up to date

  1. Import Employee History from Employee Central
  2. Trigger Basis Calculation
  3. Bonus Payout
  4. Update all worksheets

This is working well for the most part, however we have noticed that the Basis Calculation that populates Target Amount in Employee History is not calculating if an employee has had an EC change outside of the Bonus Start/End Dates. The only way to get the target amount to populate is to navigate to Employee History, tick that specific user and then click 'Update Selected'

Here are some more details below/an example.

  • Bonus Start Date: 01/07/2025
  • Bonus End Date: 31/12/2025
  • Worksheet Effective Date: 12/03/2026 (this is future dated so that employee details are current in the worksheet)
  • Global Eligibility Rule Effective Date: 19/01/2026 (this was the date we first ran the Import Employee History from Employee Central
  • Example employee on 29 January AM has a target amount of $1930, on 29 January PM the employee has a Line Manager change in EC effective 29 January, on 30 January AM when the above jobs run the target amount changes to zero. Try to trigger basis calc again but no change, only able to update by ticking this employee in the history and 'Update Selected'

Do you have any idea why EC changes outside of our Bonus Start and End Dates and impacting the ability for basis to calculate/populate?

Thank you!

entry.title: SAP Business AI & Sustainability: WHY
entry.content:
How AI Powers Sustainable Business Transformation

Sustainability is no longer optional—it’s a business imperative. With over 600 global sustainability reporting regulations and growing stakeholder demands, companies must act now. AI is a game-changer, helping businesses turn sustainability challenges into opportunities.

AI’s Impact on Sustainability

  • Emissions Reduction: AI can cut global greenhouse gas emissions by 5-10% by 2030, supporting the 1.5°C climate goal.
  • Healthcare Advancements: AI improves cancer diagnosis accuracy by up to 36.1%, enhancing patient outcomes.
  • Food Waste Prevention: AI-driven inventory optimization could prevent 907,372 tons of food waste annually, saving $2 billion.

How Executives Can Leverage AI

  • CFOs: Automate ESG reporting and risk management for compliance and cost savings.
  • COOs: Optimize supply chains for lower emissions and waste.
  • CPOs: Screen suppliers for ESG compliance and sustainable sourcing.
  • CSOs: Track carbon footprints and embed sustainability across the business.

Overcoming Sustainability Challenges Despite its potential, sustainability efforts face hurdles:

  • Data Silos: Fragmented data slows decision-making.
  • Manual Processes: Inefficient workflows increase errors.
  • Complex Insights: Distributed data makes actionable insights hard to find.

AI helps by automating data consolidation, improving accuracy, and providing real-time insights—freeing teams to focus on impact.

The Bottom Line AI isn’t just a tool—it’s a catalyst for sustainable growth. By integrating AI into sustainability strategies, businesses can drive efficiency, compliance, and positive global impact.

entry.title: Clarification on Row Type "NORMAL" behavior
entry.content:
I am currently studying SAP Data Services (BODS) and have a question regarding how the Operation Code (Row Type) changes throughout a Dataflow. Specifically, I am confused about the different behaviors of the "NORMAL" flag depending on whether a Table_Comparison (TC) transform is used.

Based on the official documentation, I understand that:

  1. All rows extracted from a source are initially flagged as NORMAL.

  2. When a row flagged as NORMAL is loaded into a target, it is treated as a new row and an INSERT operation is performed.

I have designed a test Dataflow as shown in the attached image to compare two scenarios where the source and target data are identical (no changes).

 

Scenario 1: Source -> Map_Operation (MO) -> Target (DW_DATA_COMP2)

  • MO Setting: Normal to Normal.

  • Result: It triggers a Unique Constraint Violation error because the NORMAL flag from the source is treated as an INSERT. I understand this part as it aligns with the documentation.

Scenario 2: Source -> Table_Comparison (TC) -> Map_Operation (MO) -> Target (DW_DATA_COMP)

  • TC Setting: Comparing Source and Target.

  • MO Setting: Normal to Normal.

  • Result: The rows are Discarded (no SQL is executed) by the target.

My Questions: In Scenario 2, how is each row flagged at each stage of the flow when the source and target data match exactly?

  • Source Table Stage: Is the row flagged as NORMAL?

  • Table_Comparison Stage: Does the TC transform maintain the NORMAL flag because it's a "Match with no difference"?

  • Map_Operation Stage: If the MO receives a NORMAL flag and outputs it as NORMAL, why does the Target treat this differently compared to Scenario 1?

Does the Table_Comparison transform add some internal metadata or "instruction" to the NORMAL flag that tells the Target to discard it instead of inserting it? I would appreciate a clear explanation of how the engine distinguishes between these two "Normal" states.

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding Custom Tax Engine Integration and TDT Support in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private
entry.content:
1. Background Our client is a global enterprise operating in over 100 countries. We have developed a proprietary in-house Tax Engine.

  • B2C Business: Tax calculation is handled at the front-end sales system by calling the Tax Engine directly.

  • B2B Business: We plan to trigger the Tax Engine within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition to complete tax calculations for Sales Orders (SO).

2. Key Concerns & Limitations The client is concerned that the standard SAP tax configuration may not support their complex global requirements due to:

  • a. Tax Code Constraints: Standard tax codes (2 characters) have a 1-to-1 mapping with tax types/rates. Managing validity periods at the tax code level is restrictive (validation issues), making it difficult to handle multi-tax/multi-rate scenarios across 100+ countries.

  • b. Granularity: Difficulty in accommodating highly granular tax management and business classification requirements.

3. Evaluated Integration Approaches

  • Approach A (External Suite Model): Similar to Thomson Reuters/ONESOURCE. Using custom programs to sync MM/SD/FI document data to the Tax Engine and returning results to SAP via dynamic condition records.

  • Approach B (India GST Model): Leveraging a framework similar to the 2017 India GST solution. We need SAP to detail this architecture and any potential business/product limitations.

4. Specific Questions on TDT (Time-Dependent Taxes) We understand that TDT is primarily available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Does the Private Edition fully support TDT for all 100+ target countries?

  • Can TDT resolve the aforementioned limitations regarding tax code exhaustion and validity date validation in a global rollout?

5. Request for Guidance

  • Could SAP provide architectural best practices for integrating a 3rd-party tax engine in a Private Edition environment?

  • Which approach (A, B, or a modern API-based service like SAP BTP Tax Service) is recommended for a global enterprise of this scale?

entry.title: HotNews3685270影響確認
entry.content:
お世話になっております。

■経緯
System recommendation情報にて件名HotNewsを確認

■環境
Solution Manager ST 720 0009

■確認依頼
①上記SolManシステムは、本HotNewsの対象であり、回避策はない（ノート適用もしくはSP適用が必須である）認識に間違いないでしょうか。

②SolMan以外に本HotNews対象のシステムはない認識で間違いないでしょうか。

③対応としてノート適用を実施する場合、DIGITSの後にDIGITSも必要（どちらかだけでは足りない）で認識あっているでしょうか。

④対応としてノート適用を実施する場合の影響についてご教授願います。

⑤ノート適用を実施する場合、再起動を含み、他に必要な対応はありますでしょうか。

⑥本HotNewsについて、外部攻撃懸念はない認識で間違いないでしょうか。


以上、よろしくお願いいたします。

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HotNews3685270影響確認
entry.content:
ご担当者様

平素よりお世話になっております。

お問い合わせにつき、下記回答させていただきます。

■確認依頼
①上記SolManシステムは、本HotNewsの対象であり、回避策はない（ノート適用もしくはSP適用が必須である）認識に間違いないでしょうか。

＝＞はい、ご認識の通りです。

②SolMan以外に本HotNews対象のシステムはない認識で間違いないでしょうか。

＝＞はい、ご認識の通りです。

③対応としてノート適用を実施する場合、3668705の後に3685270も必要（どちらかだけでは足りない）で認識あっているでしょうか。

＝＞あっていまま。ノート3668705と3685270両方ともご適用ください。

④対応としてノート適用を実施する場合の影響についてご教授願います。

＝＞システム動作及びビジネス運営などに影響しません。どうぞご安心ください。

⑤ノート適用を実施する場合、再起動を含み、他に必要な対応はありますでしょうか。

＝＞再起動などはいらないです。

⑥本HotNewsについて、外部攻撃懸念はない認識で間違いないでしょうか。

＝＞はい、外部攻撃懸念はないです。

以上です。宜しくお願い致します。
SAPサポート

entry.title: Re: Returned Quantity Field Does Not Allow Decimal Values in Perform Maintenance Job App in FIORI
entry.content: If the field in the CDS view or the OData entity is mapped to a data element without decimals (like MENGE vs. a whole number type), the Fiori UI will automatically mask the input to integers only.

entry.title: How to find the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting (ID: F3172) ?
entry.content:
The app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting (ID: F3172) is not visible in the Fiori Launchpad search.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to find the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Report...
entry.content:
Firstly, the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting is integrated as an add-on feature within the app Run Statutory Reports. Consequently, it does not possess its own dedicated Fiori tile, which leads to its absence in the Fiori Launchpad search.

Secondly, to locate the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting within the app Run Statutory Reports, the following requirements must be met: 

  1. Since the app Manage Tax Items for Legal Reporting is a licensed feature of DRC - Statutory Reporting, activating scope item 5XU is necessary.
  2. Activation of this feature for the relevant country is required through the configuration activity Activate Document and Reporting Compliance Features (ID: 105518).
  3. After activation, updating the reporting task is necessary in the app Run Statutory Reports.

entry.title: Standard algorithm 001 finding matches outside defined parameter sets
entry.content:
Hi,

We are having an issue where some bank transactions are being automatically allocated to the wrong customer account.

We had this issue a while ago and believed this was because interpretation algorithm 001 was searching for unspecific references on the customer accounts (e.g. the word "payment") so we limited the reference document number range under Define Parameter Sets as per below:

This seems to have somewhat reduced the problem but I'm told we are still "randomly" getting transactions incorrectly allocated to the wrong customer accounts by interpretation algorithm 001, e.g. in the below case:

From a quick glance I believe these transactions are being allocated based on the fact that the word "payment" appears in the bank statement memo and as a reference on the customer accounts.

-Is there anything that would cause the algorithm to ignore the range specified in the defined parameter sets and search for components of the bank statement memo outside of this range? We are uploading the bank statements using "Manage Incoming Payment Files" not "Upload Bank Statements". Is there something we haven't configured correctly?

-Is there a way to identify exactly which string the algorithm used to match the bank transaction to the wrong customer account? The log in Reprocess Bank Statement Items doesn't give enough detail.

Thanks

entry.title: 【SAPパートナー必見】Joule for Consultants(J4C)検討の完全ガイド
entry.content:
こちらのブログは、Finn Backerさんのこちらのブログを参考に作成したものです。

― 2026年より、Development Fundの活用も可能に ―

【2026年1月15日アップデート】
「SAP Development Fund（DF）を使って、J4C向けのAI Unitsを購入できるのか？」という質問を多くいただいています。結論から言うと、2026年より可能になりました。詳細は本文をご覧ください。

Joule for Consultants（J4C）が、すべてのパートナーに提供開始

Joule for Consultants（以下 J4C）は、SAPが昨年にローンチした Per User Per Month（PUPM）価格体系の一部として、すべてのSAPパートナーが購入・利用可能になりました。

すでにSAP社内のコンサルタントは長期間にわたってJ4Cを活用しており、
生産性向上・ナレッジアクセスの高速化・品質向上といった大きな効果が報告されています。（詳細はこちらの資料の7〜9ページをご参照ください）
日本語資料は、じゃぱぽ Business AIページからご確認ください。
(じゃぱぽへ初めてアクセスされる方は、こちらからご登録ください)

特に注目すべきは、
小規模パートナーでも大きな初期投資なしで始められる価格設計になっている点です。

パートナーからよく聞かれる5つの質問

Q1. SAP AI Unitsはどうやって購入しますか？

方法は主に2つあります。

① SAP Storeから購入（条件付き）

以下の条件を満たす場合のみ可能です：

  • 過去に 旧AI Unitを購入したことがない

  • 購入履歴が SKU 8019164 のみ

該当する場合は、SAP Storeで「Buy as a customer」を選択して購入できます。

② SAPのAccount Executive（AE）経由で購入

  • SAP AE に見積を依頼

  • Partner Business Manager（PBM）に相談してもOK

必要なAI Units数の目安

  • J4C：1ユーザーあたり 月35 AI Units

  • 例：1ユーザー × 12か月 = 420 AI Units

  • AI Unitsは100単位販売のため → 500 AI Units の購入が必要

Q2. Joule for ConsultantsにTDD（Test / Development / Demo）はありますか？

ありません。理由は明確です。

TDDは本来、

「パートナーが顧客向けSAPソリューションを検証・デモするための環境」

一方J4Cは、

パートナー自身が“本番業務”で使うツール

つまり、
パートナー自身がSAPの“顧客”として利用する位置づけになります。
そのため、J4Cは 本番（Productive）価格表のみで提供されています。

Q3. SAP Development Fund（DF）を使ってJ4Cを購入できますか？

はい。できます。

2026年1月より、SAP Development Fundを使って
J4C向けのSAP AI Units購入費用の一部をリインバース可能になりました。
ただし、SAP Development Fundが利用可能なパートナー限定となります。

⚠️ 重要な注意点

  • AI Units購入前に、必ず PBMへ事前相談・事前承認を行ってください

  • DF適用条件や手続きはパートナーごとに異なるため、詳細はPBMに確認してください

Q4. J4Cを使うために、SAPの業務システムを本番稼働させる必要はありますか？

不要です。

J4Cは スタンドアロンで動作します。

  • SAP BTPのサブアカウント上にインストール

  • コンサルタントは 専用URL から直接アクセス

  • SAPの他の本番／TDDシステムは不要

Q5. グローバルで1つのJ4Cを使えますか？それとも拠点ごとに必要？

グローバルで1インスタンス運用が可能です。J4Cは他システムと独立しているため、

  • グローバルBTP上で1インスタンス運用

  • 世界中のコンサルタントが同じURLにアクセス

ただし、

  • IAS（Identity Authentication Service）でのユーザー管理は必要です

J4Cの最適な利用対象者は？

主なターゲットは以下です：

  • 機能コンサルタント

  • 技術コンサルタント

  • SAP導入・運用支援を行うコンサルタント

活用例

  • ジュニアコンサルタント：
    → シニアに聞かずに即時に知識取得、学習スピード向上

  • シニアコンサルタント：
    → ピンポイントな情報検索、新領域対応、最新情報のキャッチアップ

J4Cを顧客に販売することはできますか？

はい、可能です。

  • パートナーは SAP AI Units（SKU 8019164） を顧客に販売可能

  • 顧客は SAP for Me からJ4Cのアクティベーションを実施

社内IT部門やパワーユーザーを抱える顧客にとっても、大きな価値があります。

まとめ：すべてのSAPパートナーが、今すぐ始められる

Joule for Consultantsは、

  • 小さく始められる

  • 月単位で柔軟に使える

  • DFも活用できる

  • グローバル展開も可能

すべてのSAPパートナーにとって、現実的かつ効果的な投資対象です。

導入にあたっては、以下を理解しておくことが重要です：

  • AI Unitsの購入方法

  • J4Cを稼働させるBTP環境の選定

  • SAP for Meでのアクティベーション

  • AI Unitsの消費モデル

  • SAP BTP上でのセットアップ方法

これらの詳細な手順については、こちらのガイド資料をご参照ください。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition：高级外币评估 (Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation)
entry.content:
业务背景

在业务上，我们都了解到大部分企业或多或少都会发生一些外币交易，那么随着货币汇率的不断波动，为了在期末能够按照即期汇率出具更准确的本位币报表或者集团货币报表就需要执行外币评估。在以前的On-Premise系统时代，大家普遍使用的是经典外币评估系统功能去实现这一业务需求，但步入云ERP系统时代后，随着并行会计核算和多币种会计核算方案的推出，经典外币评估方案就显示出一些明显的瓶颈，于是SAP在近几年推出了高级评估解决方案。

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 系统在前几年推出的高级评估解决方案中包括了“过账长期资产和负债的贴现”，“高级外币评估”，“过账基于信用风险的减值”和“过账资产负债表重新分类”四部分功能，本文旨在系统介绍高级评估解决方案中的“高级外币评估”功能。与经典外币评估相比，高级外币评估带来以下显著变化和优势：

支持并行会计核算（UPA – Universal Parallel Accounting）

支持多币种会计核算，例如功能货币，公司代码货币和集团货币

在会计报表版本中应用语义标记（Semantic Tags）概念和功能

支持直接过账至原始科目，包括未清项管理科目和统御科目

 

系统配置

激活高级评估

Activate Advanced Valuation (SSCUI 103315)

所有SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition新配置（provisioning）系统都默认激活高级评估，一旦系统存在过账数据后不得再取消激活高级评估。

 

定义用于在总账流程中确定科目分配的规则

Define Rules for Determining Account Assignments in G/L Processes (SSCUI 104827)

该配置路径中SAP预设置的配置内容不能新增，更改和删除。但您可以创建和复制自定义的科目分配规则。

 

将科目分配规则分配到高级评估

Assign Account Assignment Rules to Advanced Valuations (SSCUI 104826)

 

定义高级外币估值的规则

Define Rules for Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation (SSCUI 105450)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition系统中默认交付了以下2个评估规则。您也可以在此配置路径中创建自定义外币评估规则，并应用符合您业务需求的评估方法。

SKDV – 关键日期外币估值：默认应用始终评估（ALWAYS_VALUATE）的评估方法。

STDV – 时间相关外币估值：默认应用德国会计现代化法案（DE_BILMOG_VALUATE）的评估方法。即对于剩余期限少于1年的所有当前未清项目采用ALWAYS_VALUATE方法；对于剩余期限大于1年的非当前项目采用STRICTLY_LOWEST_VALUE（严格最低价值原则）方法。

 

在这一配置页面可以设置执行高级外币评估的汇率类型和评估方法。

 

在这一配置页面可以设置执行高级外币评估的语义标记。您也可以新增自定义语义标记后将其添加为执行高级外币评估的前提条件。

 

在这一配置页面可以设置执行高级外币评估的汇兑损益过账科目。

 

将外币估值规则分配到会计准则

Assign Rules for Foreign Currency Valuation to Accounting Principles (SSCUI 105451)

 

分配财务报表版本到会计准则

Assign Financial Statement Versions to Accounting Principles (SSCUI 103215)

这一配置将财务报表版本分配至会计准则，后续系统会按照分配的财务报表版本执行高级外币评估。

 

管理全局层次结构

Manage Global Hierarchies (F2918)

这一配置将执行高级外币评估的语义标记分配至具体的科目，包括统驭科目和总账科目。

 

经典评估补充配置

如果您还在使用经典会计报表版本配置，且未能将会计报表版本迁移至全局层次结构，请补充参考以下配置清单来完成高级外币评估的系统设置：

定义会计报表版本 Define Financial Statement Versions (SSCUI 102669)

分配财务报表版本到会计准则 Assign Financial Statement Versions to Accounting Principles (SSCUI 103215)

将语义标记分配到财务报表版本 Assign Semantic Tags to Financial Statement Versions (SSCUI 102659)

 

总结

综上所述，高级外币评估重新设计了其整个配置模型，拥有完全不同的配置路径，其配置逻辑大致为：评估规则 > 语义标记 > 财务报表版本 > 会计准则 > 评估规则。更多配置信息请参考 SAP Help Portal 高级外币评估的配置要求检查清单 和 SAP KBA 3219200 - How to Post Advanced Foreign Currency Valuation。

 

功能演示

维护期末汇率

系统应用：货币汇率 (F3616)

 

执行高级外币评估

系统应用：计划总账作业 (F1927)

高级外币评估需要分账套和公司代码执行。如果公司代码数量较多，为减少人工操作，建议创建重复运行作业。动态日历年度/期间“选项为创建重复作业提供可能性。当创建重复运行作业时，通常需要设置“动态日历年度/期间“，例如“本月最后一天”等选项，您也可以根据业务需求创建自定义动态日期选项。自定义动态日期选项请参考Fiori 应用“管理日期函数 (F2595A)” 相关功能。

 

显示高级外币运行结果

系统应用：分析关账运行结果 (F3753)

测试运行

高级外币评估有其默认的分组规则，如下图所示，未清项管理科目按照“日记账分录编号/会计年度/行项目编号/币种”分组；非未清项管理科目，如果是现金科目，则按照“开户行/开户行账户/币种”分组，如果不是现金科目，则以“科目编号”作为分组的标准之一。

 

正式运行

 

查看日记账分录

系统应用：管理日记账分录 (F0717A)

高级外币评估支持过账至原科目，无论其是否为统驭科目。按照下图显示，以供应商统驭科目 (K)的原始过账为例，虽然由高级外币评估触发过账至原统驭科目，但其科目类型已由供应商统驭科目 (K) 转换为总账科目 (S) ，且该科目依然会带有供应商信息。

 

基于本文的测试用例，高级外币评估将从交易货币 (00) 至公司代码货币 (10) 的外币评估定义为直接评估。不同系统的具体情况取决于系统中的货币设置，相关配置路径是“定义分类账和公司代码的货币设置”(SSCUI 103379)。

 

高级外币评估引入重新计量评估概念。基于本文的测试用例高级外币评估将从公司代码货币 (10) 至集团货币 (30) 的外币评估定义为重新计量评估。不同系统的具体情况取决于系统中的货币设置，相关配置路径是“定义分类账和公司代码的货币设置”(SSCUI 103379)。

 

区别于之前的经典外币评估，高级外币评估新增支持功能货币的外币评估。高级外币评估使用凭证货币对功能货币进行评估。如果尚未定义功能货币，则对公司代码货币进行评估。

 

经典评估VS 高级评估

基于前文内容，我们已经知道高级外币评估在系统功能方面相较于以前大家所熟知的经典评估有着许多大的变化。首先是运行方式的彻底改变，高级外币评估采用作业运行的方式实现，这缓解了对程序在线运行性能的要求；其次是报表显示的显著优化，以前的经典评估只能查看事实运行的结果，而高级外币评估得益于作业运行方式的实现，可以保留其历史运行结果，以便后续查看，且基于Fiori技术开发的报表，其用户体验也有较大提升；最后是评估逻辑的巨大变化，高级外币评估只采用增量评估的方式过账，对行项目级别数据进行评估，引入重新计量评估，过账至原始科目，且支持功能货币评估等。接下来，我们来简单总结一下经典外币评估和高级外币评估的差异内容：

 

经典评估

高级评估

系统应用是执行外币评估 (FAGL_FCV)

系统应用是计划总账作业 (F1927)

选择画面参数复杂

选择画面参数简单

基于评估范围和会计准则执行过账

基于分类账和会计准则执行过账

手工选择需要评估的科目

通过语义标记选择需要评估的科目

支持冲销评估和增量评估

仅支持增量评估

在科目余额层面汇总评估

对行项目数据进行评估

除汇兑损益科目外，统一过账至调整科目

除汇兑损益科目外，统一过账至原科目

通过配置基于货币类型进行评估

引入重新计量评估，默认对并行货币进行评估

不支持功能货币评估

支持功能货币评估

自动带出固定格式的抬头文本

日记账分录无抬头文本

自动带出所有行项目的固定格式项目文本

仅原科目过账自动带出固定格式项目文本

可通过配置更改外币评估的日记账分录类型

只能通过专家配置更改外币评估的日记账分录类型

支持重估应付暂估 (GR/IR) 科目

不支持重估税科目和应付暂估 (GR/IR) 科目

支持税科目重估 (SAP KBA 1589419)

不支持税科目重估

 

更多高级评估的限制请参考 SAP KBA 3290794 -Restrictions for Advanced Valuation in Financial Accounting Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition。

截止到2026年12月SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 系统中的经典评估相关功能依然可用，但还是建议客户尽快在新的财年开始之初执行系统功能切换。经典评估功能的具体弃用时间表请参考：3686385 - Deprecation Timeline for Classic Foreign Currency Valuation functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Introducing AI-Assisted Multi-Model Data Extraction in SAP Ariba Invoicing
entry.content:
Why Invoice Data Extraction Matters

Today’s invoices come in many formats and languages, and extracting accurate data quickly is critical to keeping accounts payable flowing. As SAP Ariba Invoice enhances its existing invoice capture and data extraction capabilities already in use, such as SAP Document AI, SAP is constantly focusing on innovation and improvement in content data extraction. With this release, SAP Ariba Invoicing has added three advanced large language models—Anthropic Claude Sonnet, Google Gemini Flash, and SAP OpenAI GPT (which are all connected via SAP Core AI)—into the content extraction service. This multi-model approach improves extraction accuracy, reduces manual intervention, and helps accelerate end-to-end invoice processing.

Business Impact at a Glance:

  • Higher extraction accuracy: Combining multiple state-of-the-art models improves the system’s ability to identify and extract key invoice fields (supplier, invoice number, totals, tax information, line items, etc.) across a wider variety of invoice layouts and languages.
  • Fewer exceptions and manual corrections: Better initial data capture reduces the volume of invoices that require human review, lowering processing time and overall cost per invoice.
  • Faster throughput: Improved extraction quality means invoices move through validation and approval workflows quicker, helping you shorten your days payable outstanding and improve supplier relationships.
  • Scalable and flexible: The multi-model service is designed to handle high volumes and diverse document information, making it suitable for enterprises with complex supplier networks.

How the AI-Assisted Service improves Invoice Data Capture:

  • Intelligent model integration: By leveraging multiple models, the Invoice Data Capture service can better handle varied data elements and edge cases that a single model might struggle with.
  • Confidence-driven processing: Extraction results include confidence indicators, helping the system route low-confidence invoices to human reviewers while auto-processing high-confidence extractions.
  • Continuous improvement: Feedback from corrections and validations helps refine extraction performance over time, so accuracy improves as the system learns from real-world usage.

Security, Compliance, and Data Governance:

SAP Ariba Invoicing continues to keep enterprise data protection top of mind. The AI-Assisted Extraction is integrated into the SAP Ariba Invoicing environment under existing data governance and controls. For customers with specific regulatory, residency, or contractual requirements, SAP can provide details on how the feature aligns with your compliance needs and configuration options.

Getting Started with AI-Assisted Invoice Data Extraction:

The feature is available as part of the invoice capture updates in the latest release of SAP Ariba Invoicing. To enable or configure AI-Assisted Multi-Model Extraction, check your product release notes and administration guide or contact your SAP representative or implementation partner for assistance. Your team can pilot the enhanced capture feature on a subset of invoice streams to measure improvements before wider rollout by first enabling this feature in their test environments. Then when satisfied at the results, can be enabled in their production environment.

Final Perspective:

Bringing Anthropic Claude Sonnet, Google Gemini Flash, and SAP OpenAI GPT (which are all connected via SAP Core AI) into SAP Ariba Invoicing’s capture pipeline represents a significant step forward in applying the latest AI models to real-world finance operations. The result is a more accurate, efficient, and reliable invoice processing experience that helps finance teams spend less time on exceptions and more time on strategic work.

entry.title: Secure Parameter in AEM( Advanced Event Mesh)
entry.content:
Introduction:

A Secure Parameter is an encrypted configuration value used to store sensitive data (passwords, API keys, tokens and Client secrets). so, they are not stored or displayed in plain text. Advanced Event Mesh powered by Solace Pubsub+, hence secure parameter follows the same model.

 

 


Here in AEM (Advanced Event Mesh) Secure Parameter is a field used as password alias in the Advanced Event Mesh adapter configuration which only works while selecting the authentication type as Basic. 

 

How Secure Parameter Works:

  • Define the secure parameter once.
  • Advance Event Mesh encrypts it at rest.
  • Reference it by name in the configuration.
  • The actual value is never displayed again.

This can be achieved through below Example.

Navigate to the Monitor section in Integration Suite and click on the Security Material tile 

 

Click on the Create and select Secure Parameter option

 

After selecting the Secure Parameter, you will find the below fields 

Generally, we use name as Username in most of the times for our understanding, we can give any name.


Here Secure Parameter is nothing but the password we get after configuring the Advanced Event Mesh Account, you can use another blog for how to setup the Advanced Event Mesh there you can find the password which is used to connect to the Advanced Event Mesh.

 

 

After filling the password and click Deploy the changes will now be available in the Security Material.

We will now use this secure parameter in Advanced Event Mesh. The connection Parameters are same in both Sender and Receiver Adapter.

We need to the select the authentication type as Basic for using the Secure Parameter

 

 

Conclusion:
In Summary, Secure Parameters provide a Robust and secure way to handle sensitive credentials such as passwords and secrets without exposing them in Plain text. This blog walked through the end-to-end process from creating a Secure Parameter in the Integration Suite to using it in the Advanced Event Mesh with Basic Authentication highlighting how this approach simplifies credential management while align with Solace Pubsub+ security Standards. Overall, Secure Parameters help enforce best practices for secure configuration and reduce the risk of accidental credential exposure.

Happy Learning!  See you in next the blog.

entry.title: Profit Center in SAP S/4HANA Service
entry.content:
Profit Center is a critical attribute of Finance and Controlling for Service processes as it directly affects cost and revenue posting which ultimately impact financial reporting and profitability analysis.

In SAP S/4HANA Service, the profit center attribute is supported in following service transactions:

  • Service Orders
  • Service Contracts

This blog covers the following:

1. Significance of item-based accounting for Service and profit center

2. Locating the profit center field in the UI

3. Profit center determination

4. Profit center changeability

5. Profit center exclusion

6. Profit center in follow-up transactions

 

1. Significance of item-based accounting for Service and profit center

Item-based Accounting: Item-based accounting is a concept to enable assignment of costs and revenues in Financial Accounting and Controlling to account assignment objects (also known as controlling object or CO object) that are related to the originating service order items and service contract items.

If item-based accounting is active for your service transaction scenario, profit center is mandatory for an item.

However, if the item-based accounting is not active, then profit center has no relevance in the service transaction. This means neither profit center can be determined, nor it can be entered manually for an item.

You can enable item-based accounting in Customizing using the following path:

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Enable Item-Based Accounting for Service.

 

2. Locating the profit center field in the UI

  • Profit center is an item-level attribute, which plays a crucial role in financial accounting.
  • For a Service Manager, the field is visible as 'Profit Center' in the Items tab on the service transaction as shown below:

 2.1. Service Contracts:

It is also visible in the item overview page, in the financial attributes tab.

2.2. Service Orders:

It is also visible in the item page, in the financial attributes tab.

 

 3. Profit center determination

The system determines the profit center on service transactions (like service order, service contract) by checking the following sources in a sequence of decreasing priority:

1. Substitution rule

2. Manual entry

3. Employee responsible

4. Default Configuration in Customizing

This means that the system will give preference to the substitution rule followed by manual entry, employee responsible, and finally default configuration.

3.1. Substitution rule

To define a profit center substitution rule, go to the Fiori launchpad and in the App Finder, search 'Manage Substitution/Validation Rules'.

For more information on how to create a substitution rule, see Substitution/Validation for Service Documents.

 When creating a rule, you can select or unselect the Overwrite flag.

I. Overwrite flag is selected:
The profit center defined in the substitution rule will have the highest priority. This means that if this rule is set, then even a manually entered profit center will be overwritten by the profit center defined in the rule.

II. Overwrite flag is unselected:
If the overwrite flag is unselected in the substitution rule, a profit center entered manually by the user or through an API will have higher priority than the substitution rule.

3.2. Manual entry

When a service manager enters a profit center on the item via UI or API.

3.3. Employee responsible

The system determines the cost center of the employee responsible from the item and then the profit center is determined as defined in this cost center.

Get cost center from employee responsible

TXN: PA30

Then get profit center from cost center

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Controlling -> Profit Center Accounting -> Assignments of Account Assignment Objects to Profit Centers -> Assign Cost Centers

 3.4. Default configuration in Customizing

If none of the above scenarios are successful in determining the profit center, then the system uses the default profit center configured for the controlling area.

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Financial Accounting -> General Ledger Accounting -> Business Transactions -> Document Splitting -> Edit Constant for Non assigned Processes

 

 4. Profit center changeability

  • The profit center can be changed while the life cycle status of the item is in status 'Open' or 'In Process'.
  • Profit center is not allowed to change, once there is an actual cost posting against the service item.
  • If a service contract is assigned and this contract is enabled as a root item in financial hierarchy.  If you assign a service contract and if the Customizing flag 'Root Item in Financial Hierarchy' is set on the service contract item category. Such a service contract item is then considered as a leading object for revenue recognition and can receive the revenue recognition key that represents the cost-based percentage of completion method. When a service document item is assigned to such a contract, its profit center is non‑editable (display‑only). This protects consistency across the hierarchy under collective accounting.  Configuration path to check if 'Root Item in Financial Hierarchy' is enabled:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Service -> Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Define Item Categories

 

5. Profit Center Exclusions

There are certain items for which profit center can’t be entered directly and must instead be entered on a higher-level item.

5.1. Service Hierarchy

A service hierarchy is a group of service order or service quotation items (e.g., main repair with parts/labour subitems) where the accounting and controlling for the whole group is managed through a single controlling object.

The profit center must be entered only on the main item of the service hierarchy, and all subitems inherits the profit center from the main item.

The below screenshot shows an example of service hierarchy where item 10 is the main item and item 20 is the subitem. Here, item 20 has no profit center assignment.

5.2. Price Item

Price items can only be used as the subitems of service items or execution order items. When you add a price item to a service order or a service quotation, the price item inherits the account assignment object and service contract from its higher-level item. The profit center is inherited from the higher-level item.

The below screenshot shows an example where item 20 is the price item in which profit center can't be entered. The profit center for price item is inherited from the higher-level item.

 6. Profit center in follow-up transactions

The profit center is an attribute which is automatically passed from service transactions to follow-up transactions or documents as mentioned below. 

  • Billing Document Request (BDR): Once the service transaction is released for billing and the BDR is generated, then open the 'Manage Billing Document Requests' to see the profit center in the BDR.
     
  • Sales Order: Sales order is created as a follow-up transaction from service order. Open transaction VA03 to see the profit center information.

 

 

  • Debit Memo Request (DMR): In the DMR, go to the 'Item Overview' to see the profit center details.

  • Actual/Plan Posting: Go to ‘Sales Performance Plan/Actual’ app and find profit center details.

 

 

Summary

In conclusion, Profit Center is a key element for Finance and Controlling of service processes. Hence, correctly configuring and managing profit centers in SAP S/4HANA service processes ensures accurate cost and revenue posting.
